purpose: Compact remote-operations strip mounted near the bottom of GitManagerView. Shows the current branch, its upstream tracking ref, ahead/behind counts (api.gitAheadBehind), a 'Synced' or 'No upstream' badge, and a remote selector dropdown (only when more than one remote exists). Provides three primary action buttons — Fetch, Pull, Push — each with its own loading spinner and result toast. Encapsulates Push safety logic: warns before pushing to PROTECTED_BRANCHES (['main','master']), auto-prompts to set upstream when none exists ('Push & Set Upstream'), and handles non-fast-forward rejection with a hint to pull first or force push. Force push is gated behind a two-step danger dialog (forceConfirmStep 1→2) but the IPC handler currently surfaces 'Force push is not yet supported' — UI is wired ahead of backend support.
gotchas: PROTECTED_BRANCHES is hard-coded to ['main','master'] — if the project uses 'develop' or 'trunk' as a protected branch, push will not warn.
Force push UI exists but the IPC handler is unimplemented — clicking Force after the two-step dialog returns 'Force push is not yet supported by the git backend.'
Push error parsing relies on substring match for 'non-fast-forward', 'rejected', 'no upstream', 'no tracking' — wording from git changes between versions and locales (LANG=C is NOT enforced here).
Pull merge conflict detection matches 'CONFLICT', 'Merge conflict', 'not possible because you have unmerged files' — same locale risk as push error parsing.

Auto-set-upstream on push: if aheadBehind.upstream is empty, executePush(true) is called automatically (passes setUpstream=true) — without confirmation. This may surprise users who didn't expect a tracking branch to be created.
Remote selector defaults to 'origin' if it exists, otherwise the first remote in the list — saved only in local state, not persisted across reloads.
The Force push button only appears in the result message when the error text contains 'force push' — relying on backend error wording, fragile.
Confirmation dialogs are rendered inline (NOT via SafetyDialogs.jsx) — same visual style but separate implementation; they auto-dismiss only via the cancel button or backdrop click.
patterns: Result toast with auto-dismiss after 5s — type 'success'|'error'|'warning' maps to color tokens.
anyOperationRunning = pushing || pulling || fetching || gitLoading — single boolean disables ALL actions during any in-flight op.
After every remote op, refreshAfterRemoteOp re-fetches gitStatus + gitLog + gitAheadBehind + gitBranches in parallel.
Ahead/behind chips render with arrow icons (ArrowUpIcon green for ahead, ArrowDownIcon orange for behind), or a CheckIcon Synced badge.
conventions: Push button color flips: warning class on protected branches, success class otherwise — visual cue before the user even clicks.
Inline SVG icons (PushIcon, PullIcon, FetchIcon, ForceIcon, ArrowUpIcon, ArrowDownIcon, CheckIcon, BranchSmallIcon, plus DangerDialogIcon, WarningDialogIcon, SafeDialogIcon for the inline dialogs).
All IPC results checked for `result.success` before reading `result.data`; errors surface to showResult which formats them by type.

depends_on: electron/preload.js (gitStatus, gitLog, gitBranches, gitPush, gitPull, gitFetch, gitAheadBehind)
